reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that $[0,1]^\omega$ with the uniform topology is not locally compact. In the Lean formalization, `ℕ →ᵇ I` (bounded continuous functions from ℕ to the unit interval) equals the set of all functions ℕ → I, since ℕ has discrete topology (so continuity is automatic) and I = [0,1] is bounded (so boundedness is automatic). The metric instance on `α →ᵇ β` in Mathlib is the uniform (supremum) distance `sInf {C | 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ x, dist (f x) (g x) ≤ C}`, which induces exactly the uniform topology. Thus `ℕ →ᵇ I` with its induced topology is the standard encoding of $I^\omega$ with the uniform topology. The Lean statement `¬ LocallyCompactSpace (ℕ →ᵇ I)` expresses precisely the claim that this space is not locally compact.
